description_long: This object is a pan flute, composed of a series of cylindrical bamboo-like tubes of varying lengths, arranged in a row and bound together with two horizontal bands, one of which is a reddish-brown color. The tubes are open at the top and closed at the bottom, with the tops forming a diagonal line that descends from left to right. The upper band features a decorative crisscross pattern, adding a touch of visual interest to the otherwise simple design. The tubes are smooth and have a natural, light tan color, resembling traditional hand-crafted wind instruments. The overall construction appears lightweight and portable, suitable for street performances or casual music-making in urban parks. There are no visible engravings, embellishments, or mechanical keys, emphasizing its traditional, keyless design.
description: A traditional pan flute made of smooth, light tan tubes bound together with decorative bands, featuring a diagonal arrangement of pipes and a crisscross pattern on the upper band.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full array of pipes, the diagonal arrangement, and the decorative crisscross pattern on the upper band.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile and the alignment of the tubes, with the bands visible but little detail on the pipe openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the closed ends of the pipes and the bands holding them together, with the crisscross pattern still partially visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the pipes' alignment and the bands, with less detail on the openings and decorative elements.
